docs.title: Mark Gravestock
docs.text:
I’m a Software Developer who has worked in the manufacturing, financial markets, commercial property, healthcare, advertising and retail domains.

I’ve worked in many:

  • Technologies
  • Product Domains
  • Teams of different sizes and different organisational structures
  • Delivery Methods

docs.title: Andrej Karpathy — AGI is still a decade away
docs.text:
Podcast on Dwarkesh.com

Lot's of interesting ideas covered here.

He listed three ways of using AI tool to help to code

  • Completely rejecting LLMs and writing from scratch
  • Using autocomplete while remaining the architect (his preferred approach)
  • "Vibe coding" where you prompt the model to implement things and let it do the work.

On AGI

"The problems are tractable, but they're still difficult"

On AI coding tools

"The models work well for boilerplate code and things that occur frequently on the internet in training data"

docs.title: Renovate allows dependency cooldowns
docs.text: Dependency cooldowns involve delaying updates of dependencies for a period of time, ensuring that there is time for supply chain attacks to be detected and fixed. Renovate supports this feature through it's minimum release age configuration.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
minimumReleaseAge is a feature that requires Renovate to wait for a specified amount of time before suggesting a dependency update.

The renovate documentation explains exactly how this works and how to configure it. Note that as well as renovate some pacakge managers also support minimum release age. Need to check the interoperation with maven and gradle.

docs.title: References
docs.text: Renovate - Mimimum Release Age
